nodes.label: Next.js App Router uses file-based routing where the directory structure mirrors the URL structure

metadata.full_text: Next.js App Router uses file-based routing where the directory structure mirrors the URL structure
metadata.notes: Confirmed across multiple outputs including new architecture research

nodes.label: React Server Components are the default in Next.js App Router for better performance and security

metadata.full_text: React Server Components are the default in Next.js App Router for better performance and security
metadata.notes: Consistently reinforced across outputs but conflicts with Pages Router performance claims

nodes.label: Server Components should be used for data fetching, accessing APIs, and protecting sensitive operations from the client

metadata.full_text: Server Components should be used for data fetching, accessing APIs, and protecting sensitive operations from the client
metadata.notes: Reinforced by new data fetching patterns documentation

nodes.label: Client Components should be used for interactive features requiring hooks (useState, useEffect) and event listeners

metadata.full_text: Client Components should be used for interactive features requiring hooks (useState, useEffect) and event listeners

nodes.label: Enterprise audit of 32 apps migrated to Server Components showed 68% smaller client bundles, 55% faster Time to Interactive (TTI), and zero client-side data exposure

metadata.full_text: Enterprise audit of 32 apps migrated to Server Components showed 68% smaller client bundles, 55% faster Time to Interactive (TTI), and zero client-side data exposure
metadata.notes: Conflicts with lack of official controlled benchmarks

nodes.label: Case study of e-commerce site using Next.js Server Components showed 67% Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) improvement and 51% bundle size reduction

metadata.full_text: Case study of e-commerce site using Next.js Server Components showed 67% Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) improvement and 51% bundle size reduction

nodes.label: React Server Components significantly increase page load time and reduce total JavaScript bundle size by eliminating hydration and avoiding JS shipping to clients

metadata.full_text: React Server Components significantly increase page load time and reduce total JavaScript bundle size by eliminating hydration and avoiding JS shipping to clients

nodes.label: Streaming sends HTML chunks progressively to the browser as they become ready, rather than waiting for the entire page to be rendered on the server before sending

metadata.full_text: Streaming sends HTML chunks progressively to the browser as they become ready, rather than waiting for the entire page to be rendered on the server before sending

nodes.label: Reverse proxies like Nginx buffer responses by default, requiring proxy_buffering off or X-Accel-Buffering: no header to enable streaming benefits; otherwise chunked stream arrives as one blob

metadata.full_text: Reverse proxies like Nginx buffer responses by default, requiring proxy_buffering off or X-Accel-Buffering: no header to enable streaming benefits; otherwise chunked stream arrives as one blob
metadata.notes: Critical infrastructure issue confirmed in error handling research

nodes.label: Flight protocol uses a reference system with '$' prefix notation to create cross-chunk references; chunks can reference each other using this syntax

metadata.full_text: Flight protocol uses a reference system with '$' prefix notation to create cross-chunk references; chunks can reference each other using this syntax
metadata.notes: Extensively confirmed through CVE research across multiple outputs

nodes.label: Flight serialization includes a reference resolution mechanism via the 'reviveModel' function in ReactFlightReplyServer.js that maps chunk IDs to deserialized objects

metadata.full_text: Flight serialization includes a reference resolution mechanism via the 'reviveModel' function in ReactFlightReplyServer.js that maps chunk IDs to deserialized objects
metadata.notes: Confirmed through security vulnerability analysis with detailed technical documentation

nodes.label: Pages Router handles 6x more concurrent requests than App Router in heavy server-side rendering scenarios

metadata.full_text: Pages Router handles 6x more concurrent requests than App Router in heavy server-side rendering scenarios
metadata.notes: Methodology unclear; conflicts with established App Router benefits

nodes.label: 70% TTFB reduction claimed for Server Components approach

metadata.full_text: 70% TTFB reduction claimed for Server Components approach
metadata.notes: Unusually high figure not corroborated elsewhere

nodes.label: React Flight is a row-based, line-delimited, JSON-like (but not standard JSON) streaming protocol designed specifically for serializing React component trees and async boundaries, not HTML

metadata.full_text: React Flight is a row-based, line-delimited, JSON-like (but not standard JSON) streaming protocol designed specifically for serializing React component trees and async boundaries, not HTML
metadata.notes: Consistently confirmed across multiple technical analysis outputs

nodes.label: Known type tag markers include: $ (basic reference), $@ (chunk reference/promise), $# (async boundary), $B (Blob), $D (Date), $L (lazy), $F (FormData), $S (Set), $W (WeakMap), but React's official doc

metadata.full_text: Known type tag markers include: $ (basic reference), $@ (chunk reference/promise), $# (async boundary), $B (Blob), $D (Date), $L (lazy), $F (FormData), $S (Set), $W (WeakMap), but React's official documentation does not provide exhaustive enumeration
metadata.notes: Identified through security research; completeness unknown

nodes.label: Each Flight message consists of three parts: an ID, a type indicator, and a data payload, followed by a newline character, creating line-delimited rows

metadata.full_text: Each Flight message consists of three parts: an ID, a type indicator, and a data payload, followed by a newline character, creating line-delimited rows

nodes.label: React does not publish official technical specifications or RFC documents for the Flight protocol; all detailed protocol documentation has been reverse-engineered from source code commits, security an

metadata.full_text: React does not publish official technical specifications or RFC documents for the Flight protocol; all detailed protocol documentation has been reverse-engineered from source code commits, security analyses, and CVE-2025-55182 research
metadata.notes: Critical finding confirmed across multiple outputs - extraordinary documentation gap

nodes.label: Next.js recommends parallel data fetching as the primary strategy to prevent waterfalls in App Router applications, where requests at the same hierarchical level are initiated simultaneously rather th

metadata.full_text: Next.js recommends parallel data fetching as the primary strategy to prevent waterfalls in App Router applications, where requests at the same hierarchical level are initiated simultaneously rather than sequentially

nodes.label: The lowest revalidate value across each layout and page of a single route determines the revalidation frequency of the entire route, ensuring child pages are revalidated as frequently as their parent

metadata.full_text: The lowest revalidate value across each layout and page of a single route determines the revalidation frequency of the entire route, ensuring child pages are revalidated as frequently as their parent layouts require

nodes.label: Error boundaries in Next.js App Router must be Client Components ('use client'); they wrap route segments and nested children in React Error Boundaries, catching errors thrown during rendering

metadata.full_text: Error boundaries in Next.js App Router must be Client Components ('use client'); they wrap route segments and nested children in React Error Boundaries, catching errors thrown during rendering

nodes.label: Skeleton loading fallbacks with Suspense improve perceived performance by maintaining layout stability; this strategy prevents cumulative layout shift and creates content-shaped loading indicators ins

metadata.full_text: Skeleton loading fallbacks with Suspense improve perceived performance by maintaining layout stability; this strategy prevents cumulative layout shift and creates content-shaped loading indicators instead of blank screens

nodes.label: No official Vercel or Meta published controlled benchmarks comparing pure Server Components vs pure Client Components with specific TTFB/FCP/LCP millisecond measurements exist

metadata.full_text: No official Vercel or Meta published controlled benchmarks comparing pure Server Components vs pure Client Components with specific TTFB/FCP/LCP millisecond measurements exist
metadata.notes: Critical gap confirmed; articles exist but metrics inaccessible

nodes.label: Large-scale Next.js applications use route groups, nested layouts, parallel routes, and intercepting routes as primary organizational patterns to manage complexity in 100+ route projects

metadata.full_text: Large-scale Next.js applications use route groups, nested layouts, parallel routes, and intercepting routes as primary organizational patterns to manage complexity in 100+ route projects

nodes.label: React Flight chunk boundary determination algorithm is COMPLETELY UNDOCUMENTED by the React team—no official specification, RFC, source code comments, or published algorithm exists for precise fragmen

metadata.full_text: React Flight chunk boundary determination algorithm is COMPLETELY UNDOCUMENTED by the React team—no official specification, RFC, source code comments, or published algorithm exists for precise fragmentation rules, buffer size thresholds, or backpressure mechanisms
metadata.notes: Critical documentation gap for production technology

nodes.label: React Flight protocol has NO officially documented error handling specification for streaming failures, chunk corruption, or deserialization errors

metadata.full_text: React Flight protocol has NO officially documented error handling specification for streaming failures, chunk corruption, or deserialization errors
metadata.notes: All error handling knowledge comes from CVE security research

nodes.label: Concrete directory structure examples for 100+ route Next.js applications do NOT exist in publicly available sources; while architectural patterns are documented, no production-grade example with veri

metadata.full_text: Concrete directory structure examples for 100+ route Next.js applications do NOT exist in publicly available sources; while architectural patterns are documented, no production-grade example with verified 100+ routes has been published

metadata.description: App Router performance benefits vs Pages Router handling 6x more concurrent requests
metadata.resolution: App Router benefits are well-documented for general use cases; Pages Router claim may be specific edge case with unclear methodology

metadata.description: No official controlled benchmarks exist vs enterprise audit claims of 68% bundle reduction
metadata.resolution: Enterprise claims lack transparent methodology; official benchmarks remain unavailable despite Server Components being stable
